transcript.text: How are you?
Doing well?
Good.
I was making fun of Vivek of how well his background matches his outfit when we chatted earlier.
This guy.
So sharp.
Yeah.
My lighting could do some work, though.
So it's like right above my head and the shadows get over here.
So I'm still working on it.
I'm a big fan of the colors.
But the problem is I'm also a big Patriots fan.
And those are those are Miami Dolphins colors.
So I can't really sport them.
You know, I studied at Ohio State.
So I mean, not a big NFL guy, but.
Kyle, where did you grow up?
Did you grow up in Mass or?
Most of my family's in Mass in Connecticut.
I grew up in Southern New Hampshire.
So like.
You know, 30, 40 minutes from Boston.
I remember that.
Okay.
Must be nice to have grown up winning.
I grew up in Minnesota with the Vikings.
You know, I mean, there were times in my early childhood, like up until high school was not a lot of that.
Yeah.
Okay.
So humble beginnings, but like, wow, where did you guys go?
The early days I was like with my dad, I'm like, why are you watching these teams?
They're terrible.
They lose all the time.
Yeah.
Wow.
We're still in that phase for the Vikings.
Thankfully, I'm like the most pedestrian watcher.
Like it's purely like on in the background.
I have no type of emotional investment.
So it's pretty low stakes for me.
NFL clutch up here.
Yeah, I can tell.
Do you know how bikes like crash and burn in the first round or something?
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
stuck there.
Turn the knife for me.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Thanks for joining us from space.
Yeah, what a cool background.
Mara and were just talking about it.
I always encourage folks, anyone who calls it out especially, to change the background.
So it looks like we're in the same spaceship.
I don't know how cool this crowd is, but I would definitely do that if I were you.
Thank you, Look at you guys still on Earth.
Not joking.
Look at you.
Those are kind of two favorites to me.
I feel like it's always the patio where I feel like I can hear crickets and it's just lovely.
And then space.
Is there another one that you kind of rotate in or these are kind of the main two?
These are the two, but I'm always on the lookout for anything else that gets uploaded.
So I'm definitely a power user of these virtual backgrounds.
Love that.
Yeah.
that.
This is the first time I've seen this face.
I usually get a patio all the time.
yeah.
I'm happy to see you over here.
Kaitlin, by the way, any of the other Datagrid?
Oh, no.
Just me today.
Tiago, he's joining.
He's going to join late.
So, yeah.
Sorry.
could have started with that.
It's just me.
Okay.
we're happy to just have you today.
But before we just kept talking about this, Matt, I wasn't sure if we were waiting for anyone else.
No, we are not.
We'll kick it off.
So, just some content updates.
We do have 233 articles published by the end of this week.
And this week, again, we're just going to some of the older, I don't want to say older, but some of the clusters that we've already published in, just getting through some of the other ones.
It in there.
We have been working with Dominex on the new pages, getting them up, the use case and industry pages.
There was some complexity in the way he proposed the design, so he'd come back saying that it was, you know, it needs someone with far more extensive CLS development experience in Webflow.

So what we've decided is to just go with a simpler version of it, so we can still fast, most probably just the use case pages, and then go ahead with that, and then start seeing what the response is like.
And the idea is that these use case pages will reflect the open clusters that have been doing well so far.

A little bit more on that when it's on the call, but the idea is that we'll just be following the clusters that have been doing well so that we already have some sort of, okay, there's demand for this sort of thing, and that should be more signups for the update.
That is, I'm meeting with Dominic tomorrow or Wednesday, just to find myself, because there's some CMS stuff that I don't quite understand.
So once that's done, we should be able to save those pages.
Moving on to the performance update, we did a high Eclipse this week, and, you know, impressions have remained flat over the past few weeks, but it's a picked up.
The past few weeks, we had the Google update that was rolling out, and there was some volatility in terms of how the pages were behaving.
Some of them were sort of really jumping up and down in rankings and impressions, et cetera, but overall, we seem to have done well.
There don't seem to be any negative effects from the updates, which is still good news.
Like, we hadn't seen any pages that were doing well that suddenly had just crashed and burned.
Everything sort of held its position, and on a cluster level, things didn't pretty much to say.
So.
So since we've reached the end of Q1, we sort of went in and kind of dived into all the different categories we've been.
So that's what's going on there.

We've been tracking performance throughout the quarter, but then, you know, now that we've got a majority of the articles published, we have seen that, you know, basically, instruction and then AI agents, document handling and data extraction are the ones that are doing quite well.
The sales and insurance use cases are kind of built into the document extraction and AI agents.
And that's one of the reasons why we recommend including our posts, particularly use case pages.
And then, you know, details of all this is in Looker in the second page, where we've listed, like, all the clicks, the impressions, flip-through rates, and the average positions as well.
So just based on that, we're just going to dive deeper into these particular...
clusters and then just expand the content over there.
So we're going to start doing that from the middle of next week.
And all the other content is going to be scheduled.
So that's the content update.
Any questions at all that I can answer?
Any questions?
Nope.
Sounds good.
All right.
Moving on to link building.
It's in progress.
We still are working on getting a few external links for a few sources.
Some of our, we are publishing a few articles as part of our publishing schedule.
Basically, articles that are actually sent to these weeks.
So we're working on those and those should go live by the end of this week.
The other good news is that some of our other articles that we published before, they are attracting backlinks.
And I'm referring to Mainz of Dries as well.
You mentioned you had a couple updates soon.
So thank you.
so hopefully Tiago listens to the recording and it will be pleased.
So there's external backlink acquisition efforts and then there is these backlink acquisition efforts through content, which is what Vivek was referring to.
So we had four high or high links acquired just through external acquisition links throughout efforts.
So all of those things, all of those backlinks are being pointed towards the homepage with the anchor text of AI agents, agentic AI and all those things.
And then so that's all on track.
But something that we're going as a step further is just like building more and more of these links to content, which increases the backlink velocity.
So even if we are contractually obligated to produce 20 backlinks, what we have seen from other client engagements is like it comes up to 40, 50 or 60 backlinks.
And it keeps increasing.
So as we're in month three and month four onwards, you'll see more and more links being acquired through these efforts.
And really what's happening behind the scenes is these blog articles are attracting these backlinks.
Internal links from those specific articles are being pointed towards your product pages, your industry's pages.
So that's basically you're transferring link views from external links, external backlinks to internal links.
And then it really helps with the rankings improve further and further.
So right now, depending on which platform you guys look at, SEMrush or Ahrefs, your domain authority is at a certain point.
And then anything we publish, let's say three months from now with a higher domain authority, things will just rank much higher, much faster.
So the ramp up phase, the forward backlinks acquisition is like going as per plan.
So that's the graph that we posted.
So each week we're seeing significant increases in, in, Are
And those links being acquired.
And yeah, that's pasted a couple of screenshots.
Vivek, if you could expand on the above screenshot, which has blog links, the one above, the one above.
So our team's constantly analyzing this data in terms of what is attracting these links.
then our content strategy, we start to shift apart the content budget, the weekly or the monthly content budget to templatizing these blog articles and producing more and more of these.
That's kind of to do with accelerating the backend velocity that I mentioned earlier.
So yeah, that's the overall update.
And happy to answer any follow-up questions in Slack or if a separate call is needed.
Great.
That was pretty much it for the update.
Um, there's anything else, uh, we could clarify?
Um, no, I was sorry.
was like looking at, um, looking through these metrics.
Oh, have it up on my screen.
Um, no, this all looks good.
Uh, looks like things are going well.
Um, and then I know that you said you're working with Dominic on stuff for the website.
Um, is there anything else that you guys need from us right now?
Um, not right now.
think we have everything we need to get started.
So, um, maybe a week or so, or maybe, um, two weeks from now, we will need some of the videos.
I know you're updating those pages, but, um, yeah, to get more specific things.
And I am watching Tiago's LinkedIn as well, just to see if there's any videos.
He's posting anything.
Um, so that might be the only, uh,
The ask that comes through, but on the website and the things, don't foresee anything going on.
Got it.
OK.
And then did we ever schedule a date for a new date for this webinar or did we get something on the calendar with Miles or no?
Not yet.
He shared last week, if I'm recalling correctly, a new angle of a use case that was a bit more widely compelling.
He mentioned, I think he was going to record a loon.
Am I remembering that correctly, team?
That we're waiting to hear back from him on.

And I think the key just is, like, how do we work back to, like, what's a realistic date is just understanding how we can start to apply it and, like, actually kind of, like, stress test the use cases to tell, like, real story.
So once we can get, like, hands on of, like, where is it at and then start to have that back and forth.
Theoretically, if all is aligning pretty well and it's relatively well-developed, probably the shortest after that is two weeks out, I would say.
But it all depends on how much we can really stress.
The worst case is we rush it too much and then we don't have enough of a comprehensive story to tell that's compelling enough.
Although I think the use case itself sounds super interesting.
It's just like, how do we bring it to life in a variety of different ways and talk about the different ways it could be used, show examples, and then we can kind of go through that back and forth.
Last time we talked about this too, I think you had shared wanting to get to a cadence of every six weeks and you all had something else lined up.
Is that what you ended up going forward with?
No, we don't have anything lined up yet because I wanted to see when this was going to be scheduled.
So then if not, I was going to try to schedule something separate.
Like, if this is going to get pushed out even more, then I'll probably just try to schedule something once I sync with Tiago.
And I know he had a few ideas for a webinar he wanted to do, so.
Okay, cool.
Cool.
Yeah.

Yeah, I mean, I think at a certain point, too, what we should explore, and Mara, perhaps you can help keep us organized around this, is, like, starting to develop a little bit of, like, a backlog, so we're not always just, like, you know, let's figure out this one.
And then, like, the best as we can have, like, a roadmap, I think it would make everyone's lives easier to, like, you can always change.
But just to get a bit of more rhythm and consistency and make sure everyone's feeling good.
And we can loop in some other folks on our end to continue to help us with use case development and things of that nature and just ideation.
Okay, yeah, that'd be super helpful.
Especially having a roadmap that we can kind of just track it.
Cool.
Cool.
Awesome.
I think that's the only other question I had.
All right.
Easy enough.
Well, appreciate the time, Kaitlin.
Yeah.
All right.
See you all later.
Bye.
Thank you.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Datagrid and GrowthX to discuss content, SEO, and marketing progress.](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Content production on track: 233 articles published, focusing on high-performing clusters](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=221.0)
  - [SEO performance stable post-Google update; top clusters: instruction, AI agents, document handling, data extraction](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=315.0)
  - [Link building progressing well: 4 high-quality backlinks acquired, content attracting organic backlinks](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=445.0)
  - [Website updates: Simplifying use case pages design due to complexity, focusing on top-performing clusters](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=241.0)

## Topics

### Content Strategy and Performance

  - [233 articles published by week's end](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=223.0)
  - [Focusing on previously published clusters](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=228.0)
  - [New use case and industry pages in development (simplified design)](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=241.0)
  - [Top-performing clusters: instruction, AI agents, document handling, data extraction](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=372.0)
  - [Sales and insurance use cases integrated into document extraction and AI agents](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=390.0)
  - [Detailed performance metrics available in Looker (2nd page)](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=402.0)
  - [Expanding content in high-performing clusters from mid-next week](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=423.0)

### SEO and Google Update Impact

  - [Impressions remained flat over past weeks](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=315.0)
  - [No negative effects from recent Google update](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=342.0)
  - [Pages maintained rankings; no significant drops observed](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=347.0)
  - [Cluster-level performance stable](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=353.0)

### Link Building Progress

  - [External link acquisition ongoing](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=447.0)
  - [Publishing articles as part of link building strategy](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=453.0)
  - [4 high-quality backlinks acquired, pointed to homepage with relevant anchor text](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=504.0)
  - [Organic backlinks increasing through published content](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=549.0)
  - [Projecting 40-60 backlinks in coming months (exceeding 20-link contract)](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
  - [Strategy: Transfer link juice from external backlinks to internal product/industry pages](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=561.0)
  - [Domain authority expected to increase, improving future content rankings](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=572.0)

### Website Updates

  - [Working with Dominic on new use case and industry pages](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [Simplifying design due to complexity in initial proposal](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=248.0)
  - [Focus on use case pages reflecting top-performing clusters](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=276.0)
  - [CMS details to be finalized in upcoming meeting with Dominic](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=308.0)

### Webinar Planning

  - [Awaiting update from Miles on new use case angle and Loom recording](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=492.0)
  - [Targeting 2+ weeks preparation time once use case details are available](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=781.0)
  - [Exploring development of content roadmap for consistent webinar scheduling](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=856.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Finalize CMS details with Dominic for use case pages](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [Continue expanding content in top-performing clusters](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [Monitor and leverage organic backlink acquisition](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=549.0)
  - [Await Miles' update on new use case for webinar planning](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=492.0)
  - [Develop content roadmap for future webinars (led by Mara)](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=856.0)
  - [Kaitlin to sync with Tiago on additional webinar ideas](https://fathom.video/share/_9GY5RiCxD_D_p6St6oY-rmW8ct-pMVD?tab=summary&timestamp=837.0)

action_items.description: Sync w/ Tiago re webinar ideas, schedule separate webinar if Miles' delayed
Develop webinar backlog/roadmap for consistent planning
